title: Open Source AI for Enterprise Workloads
modules: The Open Source AI Landscape: Models, Licenses, and What 'Open' Actually Means
Evaluating Open Source Models Against Enterprise Requirements
Deployment Architectures: On-Premise, Private Cloud, and Hybrid Approaches
Security, Compliance, and Data Governance with Self-Hosted Models
Total Cost of Ownership: Infrastructure, Talent, and Maintenance Trade-offs
Fine-Tuning and Customization for Domain-Specific Workloads
Integrating Open Source Models into Existing Enterprise Pipelines
Building an Open Source AI Strategy: Vendor Independence and Risk Management
synopsis: Closed AI vendors offer convenience but introduce dependency, opacity, and data risks that are increasingly unacceptable for enterprise workloads. This course gives technical leaders and architects the frameworks to evaluate, deploy, and govern open source models — from Llama to Mistral — as credible alternatives or complements to proprietary APIs.
rationale: As enterprises mature their AI strategies, the build-vs-buy decision around foundation models is becoming a board-level concern — and most professionals lack the practical grounding to make it rigorously.
learning_outcome: Professionals can assess open source model options against their organization's security, cost, and capability requirements and produce a deployment recommendation with supporting trade-off analysis.
